raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year>, I had an appointment with XXXX XXXX photos. They called me XXXX hour before my appointment and told me they could NOT take my pet photos at XXXX. They said they could move them to the end of the day at XXXX. I said I already gave my cats their calming medicine and it would wear off by then ; let me check. I called back XXXX minutes later and said that would NOT work. The receptionist said they had to charge me a {$25.00} cancellation fee for giving them less than a XXXX hour notice. I explained to her that they called me and canceled with less than an hour window for my scheduled appointment. I was just calling to tell her the one that they wanted to reschedule me in wouldnt work. She instantly charged my card and would not allow me to talk to a XXXX or anyone higher up. She stated they were no longer in the store. They were at the other location. I tried calling the other location a few times but no one answered. I just dont understand how Citi card could find me responsible for this and state The services or merchandise were provided, therefore, we're unable to support the billing error dispute as the reason.
